assertions.reason: Admin-only hardening surfaces could not be exercised because the real browser surface exposes no way to obtain admin claims; self-service signups remained limited to researcher-level UI. As a result, the full Settings category layout could not be opened in the browser.

Admin-only hardening surfaces could not be exercised because the real browser surface exposes no way to obtain admin claims; self-service signups remained limited to researcher-level UI. Domain allowlist add/remove persistence could not be exercised without an admin session.

Admin-only hardening surfaces could not be exercised because the real browser surface exposes no way to obtain admin claims; self-service signups remained limited to researcher-level UI. The admin diagnostics surface never rendered; direct navigation showed access denied.
Audit-event recording is not directly user-visible without the admin diagnostics surface, which remained inaccessible from the browser.
Audit-trail filter controls live on the admin diagnostics surface, which could not be reached from any browser-visible admin session.
Standardized infra error codes are presented on the admin diagnostics page, but that surface was unreachable without an admin role.
Lifecycle metrics are presented on the admin diagnostics page, but the browser surface provided no admin session to load that page.

blockers.description: Admin-only hardening surfaces could not be exercised because the real browser surface exposes no way to obtain admin claims; self-service signups remained limited to researcher-level UI.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried direct /admin/diagnostics navigation after self-service signup, but the account still saw Only admins can access workspace diagnostics.

summary: All settings/diagnostics assertions were blocked because the browser surface never provided an admin-capable session.
